No new direct Vahidi named statement on MOU terms dated May 24. No Ghalibaf X posts, no Aliabadi signals this cycle. IRGC communication discipline during active deal-closing phase may reflect apex management of public exposure.

Not contradictory: Vahidi could serve as a checkpoint in Pakistani communication channel without engaging directly on MOU terms. ISW multi-cycle deterrent-floor baseline carries as the stronger reference. Step 7b principal-validation: Vahidi-as-back-channel-interlocutor attributed to Al Arabiya characterization — alternative: role is IRGC apex deterrent-floor holder (ISW multi-cycle) rather than active back-channel participant. Discriminating evidence: Pakistani readout naming Vahidi specifically. Not available this cycle. Mark: inherited framing, not validated this cycle.

sources.name: OFAC.gov Economic Fury actions (most recent May 11)

finding_cards.finding: China MOFCOM issued a formal counter-order (May 2, T1) directing domestic firms to disregard US sanctions designating Chinese refineries under Operation Economic Fury. Most recent OFAC action is May 11 (Hengli Petrochemical and related entities). No new OFAC action dated May 24. No new Chinese public statements on Iran deal architecture. No PLA Taiwan Strait activity. T1 vs T1 standoff: US Treasury designates; Chinese MOFCOM counter-orders — neither resolved this cycle.

finding_cards.conflict_notes: MOFCOM counter-order (T1 Chinese government) vs OFAC designations (T1 US government): two government-level actions in direct conflict. Predicted adversarial-compliance dynamic under China's anti-coercion law framework. BS-4 sub-component (Chinese material support during conflict) remains active per Day 84 CNN T2 baseline; MOFCOM counter-order operationalizes supply-side protection of designated refineries. The Hengli-cascade threshold (to major Chinese banks) not yet crossed; current designations target refineries, not banking sector. Taiwan restraint signal: no PLA Taiwan Strait spike — consistent with Xi-restraint price extracted at summit.

Hormuz commercial traffic remains at ~5% of pre-war average (USNI T2, May 1); Hapag-Lloyd estimates 6-8 weeks for normalization post-stabilization; Maersk regular transits suspended (one US-escorted transit May 5 under Operation Project Freedom, paused May 6). CENTCOM May 14 statement (T1 via Breitbart): Iranian forces 'severely degraded across all domains'; proxies 'completely cut off.' Blockade enforcement level: unchanged from prior cycle.

Not contradictory. The Hormuz blockade enforcement level (5% commercial traffic, Maersk/Hapag-Lloyd suspended) confirms Fork A legal architecture remains in force under Stage-2 hysteresis framework.

sources.name: IEA Oil Market Report May 2026 (T1 — official OMR)
USNI News Hormuz commercial transit data (May 1)
WaPo (T2 — deal framework including Hormuz gradual reopening)

finding_cards.finding: IEA May 2026 OMR (T1): global supply down 1.8 mb/d in April to 95.1 mb/d; Gulf output 14.4 mb/d below pre-war levels; consecutive inventory draws of 129 mb (March) and 117 mb (April); IEA assumes gradual Hormuz resumption from June — absent resumption, full-year supply hit ~3.9 mb/d below baseline. Brent ~$103-104 (May 22 last confirmed print). Hormuz commercial traffic ~5% of pre-war average. Hapag-Lloyd: 6-8 weeks for normalization post-stabilization; regular transits suspended. No Kharg Island saturation event. Brent backwardation ($29/bbl Jul26-Jul27) unchanged.

finding_cards.conflict_notes: Trump 'Hormuz will be opened' (T1 Truth Social) vs Iran 'Hormuz stays under Iran management' (Fars, T3/T4): sovereignty-framing dispute does not negate structural tightness finding. WaPo MOU framework includes 'gradual Hormuz reopening' — the word 'gradual' is the probable bridging formulation (Iran opens under its management progressively). Structural tightness confirmed independent of sovereignty-framing dispute. If IEA June resumption assumption fails, the 3.9 mb/d baseline shortfall fires BS-15 Iran-side strangulation-transmission sub-threshold within 4-6 weeks per Goldman timeline.

sources.name: Israel Hayom May 24 (T2 — 'Israel alarmed as Iran deal takes shape')
IDF MoD F-35I Elbit contract announcement (T1, May 14)

finding_cards.finding: Netanyahu emergency security cabinet statement (ToI/Bloomberg T2): claimed Trump gave two private assurances — (a) final agreement requires full dismantlement of enrichment sites and removal of all HEU from Iran, and (b) Israel retains freedom of action against all threats in all arenas. No White House corroboration of 'full dismantlement' language. Israel Hayom: 'Israel alarmed as Iran deal takes shape.' Israeli security sources 'almost completely cut off from updates' on negotiations. Three-signal pre-emption cluster over 10 days: F-35I fuel tank contract ($34M Elbit Systems, MoD T1, May 14, 12-18 month development program explicitly targeting Tehran range without aerial refueling); Zamir 'targets ready for a powerful and broad operation' (T1 JNS, May 6); postponed scheduled attack May 18 (Trump T1). Knesset committee stage not yet scheduled. No Israeli unilateral strike.

Most parsimonious: Trump made reassuring but non-committal noises; Netanyahu translated into maximalist framing for Ben-Gvir/Smotrich coalition management. Discriminating evidence: White House readout using 'full dismantlement' language — not available this cycle. Step 7b principal-validation: alternative principal — Netanyahu is the framing agent, not Trump; Trump made no such explicit commitment. 4th+ consecutive cycle of Netanyahu claiming Trump private maximalist endorsement without White House corroboration. Flag for next /premortem wrong-principal review on Netanyahu-Penetration-mechanism characterization.

finding_cards.finding: House GOP leadership cancelled a scheduled floor vote on H.Con.Res.38 on May 21 before Memorial Day recess after Democrats and a cross-party bloc appeared to have sufficient votes to pass it; Rep. Meeks (D-NY): 'We had the votes to pass it today' (CNN/WashExaminer T2, NPR T2). House does not return until early June; WPR likely to be first major floor action post-recess. Senate 50-47 procedural advance (May 19) holds. Fetterman confirmed structural holdout on WPR (Semafor T2). White House continues to argue WPR requirements 'no longer apply' due to ceasefire.

trigger_detail.appendix_b_row: T9 Stage-2 hysteresis mechanism updated: WPR majority demonstrated but blocked by GOP floor management scheduling; qualitative change in lock-in architecture

variable_moved.new_value: Lock-in depends on GOP floor management preventing a demonstrated majority from voting; procedurally more fragile than vote-arithmetic framing suggested; majority exists and is being blocked, not absent
variable_moved.driver: GOP leadership cancelled floor vote when cross-party majority was apparent; 'we had the votes' per Meeks

finding_cards.conflict_notes: Day 84 '213-214 narrow fail' framing understated the House WPR majority risk. The failed vote was one that was allowed to proceed; the May 21 cancelled vote implies a larger majority was imminent. T9 VALIDATED holds but mechanism requires update: lock-in is primarily a function of GOP leadership floor scheduling, not vote arithmetic. Fetterman as structural holdout on Senate side confirmed — Senate on-merits vote still expected to fail at full attendance (VP tiebreaker), but the procedural advance holds. When House returns early June, WPR vote is the first structural test.

finding_cards.probe_name: MOU Framework and Resumed-Operations Trigger Tracking

sources.name: NBC News / Times of Israel (T2 — Trump 'in no rush')
IRIB / Pezeshkian statement (T1, May 24 — nuclear excluded from MOU)
Washington Times (T4 — '24 hour announcement' claim, unconfirmed)

finding_cards.finding: WaPo (T2, May 24) reports most specific MOU architecture to date: 60-day ceasefire, gradual Hormuz reopening, end to US naval blockade, Iran oil sales restored, nuclear talks deferred to a second phase. Trump walked back 'largely negotiated' (May 23 T1) to 'in no rush' (May 24 T2 ToI/NBC) within ~24 hours. Munir departed May 23; Iran issued 'mixed messages' per Al Jazeera T2; Iranian officials stated visit 'did not represent a turning point or decisive situation' and that 'deep and significant disagreements remain.' Pezeshkian (IRIB T1, May 24): Iran 'ready to assure world not seeking nuclear weapons'; nuclear explicitly excluded from current MOU scope; war-end first, nuclear addressed in final-agreement phase. Iran insists Hormuz is 'under its control' regardless of deal terms. 6th round Oman (scheduled Sunday May 24-25 per Day 86) not confirmed in search results as of May 24. Washington Times T4 '24-hour deal announcement' claim unconfirmed.

-25% on WaPo framework-details anonymous sourcing: multiple T2 outlets may share one provenance cluster; apply multi-outlet anonymous discount (Step 3b); originating cluster likely one US official familiar with framework
finding_cards.conflict_notes: Trump 'largely negotiated' (T1, May 23) vs 'in no rush' (T2 corroborated, May 24): same principal, ~24h oscillation. Per A1 mechanism, this is within the predicted oscillation pattern — 'in no rush' likely reflects post-Munir 'mixed messages' information and/or Netanyahu call effect on May 23 evening. Iran insists Hormuz is 'under its control' (Fars, T3/T4) vs Trump 'Hormuz will be opened' (T1) — dual-collision persists on sovereignty framing. WaPo framework includes 'gradual Hormuz reopening' — 'gradual' may be the bridging formulation Iran can accept (opens under Iranian management incrementally). Pezeshkian T1 nuclear-excluded-from-MOU framing is the most important Iranian T1 signal this cycle: it confirms war-end-first, nuclear-second sequencing as the operative Iranian deal architecture.

sources.name: CNN T2 US IC (Day 84 baseline — two named US officials)

finding_cards.finding: No new capability signals beyond Day 84 CNN T2 baseline (90% underground missile storage partially/fully operational per satellite imagery; drone production resumed during ceasefire; full reconstitution within six months; Russian and Chinese material support continuing). CENTCOM May 14 (T1 via Breitbart): Iranian forces 'severely degraded across all domains'; proxies 'completely cut off from weapons supply.' Timeframe distinction: CENTCOM assessed post-strike-phase outcome; CNN T2 IC assessed ceasefire-period reconstitution trajectory. Not contradictory. Houthis: retain long-range strike capability but strategic calculus constrained — significant US-target action risks organizational collapse (Stimson T3). Hezbollah: operationally marginal; Lebanese government prohibited military activity (FP/Belfer T3). IDF cross-Litani operations continuing to degrade Hezbollah infrastructure.
discounts_applied: CENTCOM 'severely degraded' statement via Breitbart (T4 outlet): underlying CENTCOM statement is T1 but filtered through T4 source; flag for direct CENTCOM.mil confirmation; treated as effective T2
finding_cards.conflict_notes: CENTCOM 'severely degraded' (post-strike assessment, ~May 14) vs CNN T2 IC 'reconstituting faster than expected' (ceasefire-period, May 21): different timeframes, not contradictory. CENTCOM was accurate at the moment of strike-phase assessment; IC reconstitution finding covers the subsequent recovery trajectory. Day 84 Slantchev feigning-weakness at H confidence (US-IC-confirmed) carries; no new confirmation or contradiction this cycle.

finding_cards.probe_name: Mutual Dispositional Reading and Power-Shift Perception

sources.name: Israel Hayom May 24 (T2 — 'Israel alarmed as Iran deal takes shape')
Times of Israel Daily Briefing May 24 (T2)
IDF MoD F-35I Elbit contract (T1, May 14)

finding_cards.finding: Netanyahu maximalist anchoring confirmed at maximum alarm level: claimed Trump gave private assurances of full dismantlement and HEU removal; Israeli security sources 'almost completely cut off from updates' on negotiations (Israel Hayom, ToI T2). Israel Hayom: 'Israel alarmed as Iran deal takes shape.' Three-signal pre-emption cluster over 10 days: F-35I fuel tank contract (MoD T1, May 14, explicitly targeting Tehran range without aerial refueling, 12-18 month program); Zamir 'targets ready for powerful and broad operation' (T1 JNS, May 6); postponed scheduled attack (May 18). Trump dispositional: 'in no rush' — deal-oriented but urgency lowered. US-Israeli dispositional divergence sustained at structural peak: Trump process-oriented and de-escalatory in public framing; Netanyahu maximum-alarm and pre-emption-incentivized.

finding_cards.conflict_notes: Trump 'in no rush' public de-escalation vs Netanyahu maximum-alarm framing: not contradictory — T8 predicts exactly this dynamic. The more credible the deal becomes, the higher the Israeli pre-emption incentive. Israel being 'cut off from updates' amplifies the Powell closing-window dynamic: Netanyahu cannot monitor deal progress in real time, raising rational incentive to pre-empt before architecture locks in an inadequate outcome. Step 7b: alternative principal — IDF Chief Zamir and General Staff, not Netanyahu, are the operative decision-makers on pre-emption timing in the caretaker period. Zamir baseline (May 6 T1): 'if uranium removed diplomatically, we have done our part' (senior IDF, T2 corroboration). Knesset committee stage not yet scheduled — Netanyahu in pre-caretaker window, retaining full operational authority.

sources.name: Composite of PROBE-2, PROBE-8, PROBE-9, PROBE-10, PROBE-12', PROBE-13, PROBE-14, PROBE-15 this cycle
Agent sourcing: Iranian Hajj exclusion (visas suspended, flights cancelled)

finding_cards.finding: Joint-distribution update from component probes. Iranian side: no rupture signals; Pezeshkian limited-aims framing (T1); coalition holding; 'deep and significant disagreements remain' (Al Jazeera T2). US side (Trump A1): 'in no rush' softening within 24h of 'largely negotiated' — consistent with A1 oscillation; 48h test partial fail. Israeli threshold: three-signal pre-emption cluster; Netanyahu maximalist anchoring; T8 at maximum loading; Knesset committee stage not yet scheduled (pre-caretaker window active). WPR threshold: House majority demonstrated but blocked by GOP floor management; elevated risk for early June return. Hajj backchannel: Iranian nationals largely excluded from Hajj (visas suspended, flights cancelled) — the ~30,000 Iranian pilgrims on Saudi soil premise underpinning the BS-18 Hajj-backchannel pending-trend candidate did NOT materialize; no confirmed Saudi-Iran principal-level meeting during Hajj. Strangulation: IEA OMR T1 confirms structural tightness; consecutive inventory draws advancing strangulation clock.

Both consistent: unified public output through different internal causal pathways (Day 84 structural finding) is compatible with UAE privately demanding harder guarantees. Divergence is not a brake fracture (T1 required for that trigger) but MBZ-pathway fragility is advancing. Hajj backchannel mechanism flag for /audit: pending-trend 'Cross-pole Saudi-Iran diplomatic geometry via Hajj backchannel' should be revised — mechanism is official-to-official contact (Saudi interior minister + Iranian Hajj official), not pilgrim-facilitated backchannel. Promotion criteria should require named official Saudi-Iranian meeting at FM level or above, not pilgrim-channel signals.

finding_cards.finding: P-AIM: Pezeshkian (IRIB T1, May 24) stated Iran 'ready to assure world not seeking nuclear weapons'; nuclear explicitly excluded from current MOU scope; demand-set bounded at war-end + sanctions relief + Hormuz management — limited aims classification maintained. Iran's Hormuz 'under our control' insistence is consistent with limited-aims bargaining (sovereign management assertion) rather than order-revisionist demand. P-DG: no new US targeting of named Iranian principals. P-DG2: Iran 'deep and significant disagreements remain' after Munir departure — demand-firmness, not post-strike demand escalation; P-DG2 requires post-strike causal framing, not applicable. P-OVEX: Houthis constrained (Stimson T3), retain long-range capability; Hezbollah operationally marginal (FP/Belfer T3) — proxy degradation accumulating across channels. P-INFO: no signals. P-CHEMO: no ground-force buildup.

finding_cards.conflict_notes: Iran's Hormuz 'under our control' framing could be read as order-revisionist (refusing US freedom of navigation) or limited-aims (asserting sovereign management while negotiating transit arrangement). WaPo MOU framework detail of 'gradual Hormuz reopening' suggests Iran is willing to negotiate transit terms — consistent with limited-aims classification. Pezeshkian T1 'nuclear weapons assurance' framing directly contradicts P-AIM unlimited reading. P-AIM limited classification maintained; P-OVEX proxy degradation is the operative Paine cost-clock advancing this cycle.

Trump 'in no rush' within 24h of 'largely negotiated' is A1 oscillation behavior — predicted. Netanyahu maximum-alarm emergency meeting simultaneous with deal-advancing is T8 Powell mechanism — predicted. WaPo framework details emerging, Pezeshkian T1 nuclear-excluded-from-MOU, Munir 'mixed messages' — all consistent with the materialist bargaining model operating under joint constraints. No thesis break. Five constraint layers conditioned each principal's decision set this cycle exactly as predicted.
